purpose: Real local-device attempt to run the official SWE-bench harness (per 'use local devices as much as possible').

attempts.evidence: 404 Client Error: No such image swebench/sweb.eval.x86_64.<instance>:latest (prebuilt images not published for these instances)
docker build -> 500 Internal Server Error: unshare: operation not permitted (build_base_images: sweb.base.py.x86_64:latest)

root_cause: runtime is an unprivileged container: CapEff=00000000a80c25fb lacks CAP_SYS_ADMIN; vfs storage driver; kernel denies the namespace creation (unshare) the OCI runtime needs to build/run containers
local_devices_used_for: Qwable real inference model gate on 4x RTX 4090
conclusion: Official SWE-bench Docker scoring is blocked in BOTH local and qz environments by unprivileged-container namespace limits (no CAP_SYS_ADMIN). Every non-Docker component runs on local devices. Official resolved rate is not claimed.
